q: The Fundamental Theorem of Arithmetic states that every composite number can be expressed as a product of:
sol: Every composite number can be uniquely factorized into a product of primes, regardless of the order.

q: Which of the following is an irrational number?
sol: The square root of a non-perfect square is always irrational.

q: If p is a prime number and p divides a², where a is a positive integer, then:
sol: By a key theorem in NCERT, if a prime p divides a², it must also divide a.

q: For any positive integer n, 6ⁿ can end with the digit 0 if its prime factorization contains:
sol: A number ends in 0 only if its prime factors include 2 and 5.

q: The sum of a rational and an irrational number is:
sol: The sum of a rational and an irrational number is always irrational.

q: The HCF of 26 and 91 is:

sol: HCF takes the lowest power of common factors: x¹ and y².

q: If a = x³y² and b = xy³, where x, y are prime numbers, then LCM(a, b) is:
sol: LCM takes the highest power of all prime factors: x³ and y³.

q: If p₁ and p₂ are two odd prime numbers such that p₁ > p₂, then p₁² - p₂² is:
sol: Odd minus odd is even. (Square of an odd is odd).

sol: 2n(2n+2)(2n+4) = 8n(n+1)(n+2). Since n(n+1)(n+2) is divisible by 6, the product is div by 48.
